clues.clue: This muscular tube uses peristalsis to carry swallowed food from the throat to the stomach.
Most nutrient absorption happens in this longest, coiled section of the digestive tract.
This large organ secretes bile, stores glycogen, and detoxifies the blood.
This small sac beneath the liver stores and concentrates bile between meals.
The first, C-shaped segment of the small intestine, where bile and pancreatic juice enter, is this.
Chronic backflow of stomach acid into the esophagus is this reflux disease, known by a three-letter abbreviation.
Cramping, bloating, and alternating diarrhea and constipation without structural damage mark this functional bowel syndrome.
The bacterium Helicobacter pylori is a leading cause of these painful open sores in the stomach lining.
This autoimmune reaction to gluten flattens the small-intestine villi, impairing absorption.
Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis are the two main forms of this chronic inflammatory umbrella condition.
Despite its name, this burning sensation behind the breastbone after meals has nothing to do with the heart.
Clinicians use this term for difficulty or pain when swallowing.
Black, tarry stool signaling digested upper-GI bleeding is called this.
This is the medical term for vomiting blood.
Yellowing of the skin and eyes from a buildup of bilirubin is this sign of liver or bile trouble.
Antacids such as Tums relieve indigestion by doing this to stomach acid.
Drugs like omeprazole sharply cut acid secretion; this class is abbreviated PPI.
Bismuth subsalicylate is the active ingredient in this pink, stomach-soothing liquid.
Surgical removal of the inflamed appendix is this common emergency procedure.
For severe reflux, the Nissen version of this surgery wraps the upper stomach around the lower esophagus.
This scope exam threads a flexible camera through the rectum to screen the large bowel for cancer.
Abbreviated EGD, this scope exam inspects the esophagus, stomach and duodenum from above.
A urea breath test detecting urease activity is used to diagnose infection by this ulcer-causing microbe.
In this X-ray series, the patient drinks a chalky contrast so the esophagus and stomach show up clearly.
A tiny camera inside a swallowable pill records this exam of the small bowel.

final.clue: Fecal microbiota transplantation can cure stubborn infections by this antibiotic-resistant gut bacterium, 'C. diff.'
